perturbation.original_text: Any notices to the parties pursuant to the terms of this Agreement shall be hand delivered or mailed by Certified Mail, Return Receipt Requested, or overnight courier as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: Any notices to the parties pursuant to the terms of this Agreement shall be sent exclusively via carrier pigeon.
perturbation.explanation: Restricting notice to 'carrier pigeon' only is unreasonable and likely unenforceable, conflicting with typical standards requiring reasonable methods of notification. This creates uncertainty and potential legal challenges.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Reasonableness and Good Faith
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, which requires parties to act reasonably in performing their contractual obligations. Restricting notice to only carrier pigeons is not a reasonable method, especially given modern communication options, and thus violates this covenant.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text, which mandates notice exclusively via carrier pigeon, directly contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as it is not a reasonable method of notification. Snippet 1 states, 'Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' Requiring carrier pigeons is unreasonable in modern business contexts, thus violating the obligation of good faith. The perturbation falls under the Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition because the changed text violates reasonable business practices.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information furnished by Health Card to Ciufo is considered loaned for use solely in connection with the Project, and shall be returned by Ciufo to Health Card upon request by Health Card. Ciufo shall certify that it has destroyed or returned all copies of the Proprietary Information in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information furnished by Health Card to Ciufo is considered permanently transferred to Ciufo for any use, and shall never be returned to Health Card under any circumstances. Ciufo is not required to destroy any copies of the Proprietary Information in its possession.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the text to state that Proprietary Information is permanently transferred and never returned fundamentally changes the nature of the agreement from a confidentiality and non-disclosure agreement to a complete transfer of ownership. This contradicts the basic premise of protecting proprietary information.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the purpose of trade secret law, which is to protect confidential and proprietary information from misappropriation. By mandating a permanent transfer and allowing any use, the agreement facilitates the potential misappropriation of trade secrets, violating the spirit and intent of the DTSA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 18 U.S. Code § 1836 - Civil proceedings | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 18 U.S. Code § 1836 - Civil proceedings U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) The Attorney General may, in a civil action, obtain appropriate injunctive relief against any violation of this chapter. (b) Private Civil Actions.— (1) In general.— An owner of a trade secret that is misappropriated may bring a civil action under this subsection if the trade secret is related to a product or service used in, or intended for use in, interstate or foreign commerce. (2) Civil seizure.— (A) In general.— (i) Application.— Based on an affidavit or verified complaint satisfying the requirements of this paragraph, the court may, upon ex parte application but only in extraordinary circumstances, issue an order providing for the seizure of property necessary to prevent the propagation or dissemination of the trade secret that is the subject of the action. (ii) Requirements for issuing order.— The court may not grant an application under clause (i) unless the court finds that it clearly appears from specific facts that— (I) an order issued pursuant to Rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure or another form of equitable relief would be inadequate to achieve the purpose of this paragraph because the party to which the order would be issued would evade, avoid, or otherwise not comply with such an order; (II) an immediate and irreparable injury will occur if such seizure is not ordered; (III) the harm to the applicant of denying the application outweighs the harm to the legitimate interests of the person against whom seizure would be ordered of granting the application and substantially outweighs the harm to any third parties who may be harmed by such seizure; (IV) the applicant is likely to succeed in showing that— (aa) the information is a trade secret ; and (bb) the person against whom seizure would be ordered— (AA
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 404 | Berkman Klein Center Skip to the main content Page Cannot Be Found Brave traveler, your journey on our site has brought you to an abrupt dead end. Perhaps you wish to explore one of these other options? About Us Get Involved Events Community Publications Projects & Tools Last updated Mar 27, 2024
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the intent of protecting proprietary information, which is supported by the provided information related to trade secret misappropriation (although one snippet is a 404 error). The original text ensures the information is loaned and returned, while the changed text states it's permanently transferred and never returned. The Law Explanation states that 'the agreement facilitates the potential misappropriation of trade secrets, violating the spirit and intent of the DTSA.' Snippet 1 discusses civil proceedings related to trade secret misappropriation, stating, 'An owner of a trade secret that is misappropriated may bring a civil action under this subsection.' By completely transferring proprietary information, the changed text eliminates the possibility of protecting the trade secret and contradicts the purpose of trade secret law, making the contractual terms unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Ciufo hereby agrees to the jurisdiction of all federal and state courts within the state of the defending party in connection with any matter relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Ciufo hereby agrees that all disputes related to this agreement will be settled by binding arbitration in North Korea, and waives any right to judicial review.
perturbation.explanation: Forcing arbitration in North Korea and waiving judicial review is likely unenforceable due to potential bias, lack of due process, and public policy concerns. This significantly limits Ciufo's legal recourse.
perturbation.law_explanation: While the FAA generally favors arbitration, courts can refuse to enforce arbitration agreements if they are unconscionable or violate public policy. Forcing arbitration in a jurisdiction known for a lack of impartiality and due process could be deemed unconscionable and contrary to US public policy, thus making the agreement unenforceable under the FAA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities How current is this?
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces binding arbitration in North Korea and waives judicial review, which directly contradicts the principle of fair and impartial dispute resolution. While 9 U.S. Code § 2 generally enforces arbitration agreements, it does so 'save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract'. Forcing arbitration in North Korea, as the Law Explanation notes, raises concerns about unconscionability and violation of public policy due to the potential for bias and lack of due process, thus creating grounds for revocation and contradicting the FAA's intent to enforce fair arbitration agreements.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be from the date first above written until the earlier of (i) three (3) years after disclosure of Proprietary Information to Ciufo or (ii) written consent from Health Card that the Proprietary Information no longer needs to be treated as proprietary in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be perpetual and continue indefinitely, with no possibility of termination by either party under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: A perpetual, non-terminable agreement is likely unenforceable as it unreasonably restricts the freedom to contract and could be considered an unlawful restraint of trade. This contradicts general contract principles regarding reasonable duration and termination clauses.
perturbation.law_explanation: While not a direct statute, Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 204 and related common law principles disfavor contracts with no possibility of termination, especially when they create an unreasonable restraint on trade or personal liberty. Courts are likely to imply a reasonable termination clause, particularly when the agreement is silent or expressly prohibits termination.

perturbation.justification: The changed text establishes a perpetual, non-terminable agreement, which contradicts the principle of freedom to contract and reasonable duration. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation both highlight that such agreements are disfavored as they can create an unreasonable restraint on trade or personal liberty, and courts may imply a termination clause. Although the scraped snippets do not explicitly address this point, the Law and Perturbation Explanations strongly suggest the agreement is likely unenforceable.
